text.title: Business Model Canvas and Revenue Archetypes
text.summary: Nine-block Business Model Canvas with Hard/Soft cell tagging and five revenue archetypes with variant tables and best-fit signals.

rule.statement: Populate all 9 Canvas blocks before naming any price; tag each cell Hard (sourced from a public filing, pricing page, or comparable company) or Soft (founder estimate).
rule.rationale: Agents skip the Canvas and produce unit economics without understanding the cost structure. Hard/Soft tagging makes the assumptions ledger auditable and enables future re-runs to diff what changed.

list.item: Customer Segments: who are the paying users? primary + secondary
Value Propositions: what value is delivered to each segment?
Channels: awareness, evaluation, purchase, delivery, after-sales
Customer Relationships: self-serve / assisted / automated; retention mechanisms
Revenue Streams: model type, price, estimated % of total revenue
Key Resources: physical, intellectual, human, financial
Key Activities: the 3 most critical activities for delivering value
Key Partners: what they provide, what they get in return
Cost Structure: fixed vs variable; cost-driven vs value-driven orientation

rule.statement: Shortlist exactly 2 of the 5 revenue archetypes that fit the value chain; reject the others on record with a one-line reason before running unit economics.
rule.rationale: LLMs default to SaaS subscription whenever the product is digital. Forcing an explicit rejection of the other four archetypes surfaces assumptions that would otherwise remain implicit.

list.item: Subscription/SaaS: flat rate, tiered, per-seat, usage-based, freemium — best for ongoing value delivery, sticky products. Key metrics: MRR, churn, LTV, CAC.
One-time purchase: digital product, software license, physical product — best for discrete value and impulse buys. Key metrics: units sold, revenue per product, repeat purchase rate.
Transaction-based: percentage cut, flat fee per transaction, hybrid — best for marketplaces, payments, brokerage. Key metrics: GMV, take rate, transaction volume.
Advertising: display, sponsored, affiliate — best for high-traffic content platforms. Key metrics: DAU, CPM, CTR, ad inventory fill rate.
Marketplace/Commission: service, product, lead generation — best for matching problems with network effects. Key metrics: GMV, take rate, liquidity.

antipattern.description: Assuming 20-30% take rate for a new marketplace.
antipattern.reason: Real successful marketplaces compress to 5-15% as suppliers gain leverage (Etsy 6.5%, Airbnb 14%, Upwork 10%). Agents assume high take rates because they pattern-match aspirational claims.

antipattern.description: Using revenue as if it were profit in the Canvas Cost Structure block.
antipattern.reason: Gross margin must be sourced separately; AI-heavy products run 30-50% due to inference costs, not 80% SaaS folklore. Missing the Cost Structure block makes LTV:CAC unreliable.

antipattern.description: Modeling freemium conversion at 15% without evidence.
antipattern.reason: Floor conversion is 2% (consumer) / 5% (B2B). Claims above these require an empirical citation or a Soft tag.
